title: galium pumilum subsp. jordanii
intro: Galium pumilum subsp. jordanii, also known as Jordan's bedstraw, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Jordan's bedstraw include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Galium pumilum subsp. jordanii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
